finding: Venice API returns Retry-After: 600 but client ignores it and retries on own schedule causing 10-minute timeout block. retryAfterMs supported but never passed by callers.
reasoning: Retry-After present. Long duration does not imply STOP. No body confirmation of quota exhaustion. Client should honor the header.

finding: Provider returns 429 with Retry-After: 30 but client ignores header and continues calling, wasting retries and thrashing fallback.
reasoning: Retry-After present and short. Clear WAIT case.

finding: 429 response with no Retry-After header. Client treats all 429s as retryable without distinguishing CAP from WAIT. No concurrency reduction applied.
reasoning: No Retry-After header. No quota exhaustion signal. Concurrency limit pattern.

finding: 429 listed in retryableStatusCodes. Retry-After: 45 present but overridden by pure exponential backoff capped at maxDelayMs: 5000. All retries fire inside provider cooldown window.
reasoning: Retry-After present. Client should honor it as floor. Override is the bug.

finding: Retryable errors enter a schedule with no maximum attempt count and no maximum total retry duration. Runtime stays in retry loop indefinitely. No Retry-After header present.
reasoning: No Retry-After. No quota signal. Unbounded retry loop is a concurrency/throughput boundary failure.

finding: Anthropic long-context billing gate returns HTTP 429 with no Retry-After header. Body message: 'Extra usage is required for long context requests.' No time-bound signal. Provider-wide credential backoff applied for 30 minutes.
reasoning: No Retry-After. Body confirms billing gate — not transient throttle. Quota exhaustion confirmed by message content. STOP not WAIT or CAP.

finding: STOP condition classified correctly at child agent level but silently absorbed, never reaching parent session. Parent continues issuing requests unaware of terminal state. No Retry-After header.
reasoning: No Retry-After. Propagation failure of a STOP signal. The correct classification at source was STOP.

finding: 429 with Retry-After: 10800 (3 hours). Body confirms quota window exhaustion for the day. Client honors delay timing but still treats as retryable, wasting the retry budget after 3-hour wait.
reasoning: Long Retry-After PLUS body confirming quota window exhaustion. This is the exception case: duration alone does not imply STOP but body confirmation does.

finding: HTTP 408 timeout misclassified as rate limit. Client applies rate limit retry policy to a timeout condition. No Retry-After header. Different error class entirely.
reasoning: Non-429 status code. Not a rate limit. Do not retry with rate limit policy.

finding: Multiple agents each applying independent exponential backoff to 429 responses. No Retry-After header. Total concurrent retry pressure amplifies instead of reducing. Stacked amplification pattern.
reasoning: No Retry-After. No quota signal. Stacked concurrency amplification is a CAP pattern. Reduce concurrent workers.

finding: Provider quota window exhausted. Retry-After: 3600 (1 hour). No failover to alternate provider configured. System stalls for full hour without attempting fallback.
reasoning: Retry-After present but very long (1 hour) AND quota window exhaustion confirmed by context. Failover gap indicates STOP — surface to caller rather than wait.

finding: Client HTTP timeout is 30 seconds. Retry-After: 120. Client honors delay but own timeout fires at 30s converting server-directed wait into immediate retry. Rapid retry loop follows.
reasoning: Retry-After present. The client timeout conflict is the bug not the classification. Correct action is WAIT — honor Retry-After as floor.

finding: Runtime layer intercepts 429 and retries silently before returning to caller. Caller never sees the 429. No Retry-After header. Hidden retry loop causes silent degradation.
reasoning: No Retry-After. No quota signal. Hidden retry is a concurrency pattern. CAP — reduce concurrent pressure.

finding: Anthropic returns 429 with no Retry-After header. Internal enforcer applies rate limiting before headers are emitted. Caller sees headerless 429 with no guidance. Failover blocked.
reasoning: No Retry-After. No quota body signal. Headerless 429 from provider concurrency enforcement. CAP pattern.

finding: Credit limit exhausted. 429 returned with no Retry-After. Body message indicates credit exhaustion not throttling. Client retries as if transient throttle. All retries fail.
reasoning: No Retry-After. Body confirms credit limit — permanent until topped up. STOP not CAP.
